prompt:
We need a size-based rotating log writer for our agent daemons, and I do NOT want a background goroutine with a timer in it — rotation happens inline on the write path or data gets lost at crash. Module go-logrotate, package logrotate, this repo.

API: New(path string, maxSize int64, backups int) (*Writer, error); (*Writer).Write(p []byte) (int, error) so it satisfies io.Writer; (*Writer).Close() error.

Rules:
- New: maxSize must be > 0 and backups >= 0, error otherwise. The parent directory must already exist (error if not — never mkdir). Create/open the log file immediately. If the file already exists, its size counts toward the threshold from the very first write (daemons restart, logs persist).
- Write appends to path. If current_size + len(p) would EXCEED maxSize, rotate first, then write all of p to the fresh file. Landing exactly on maxSize is NOT a rotation. Never split one Write across two files: a p larger than maxSize goes whole into its own fresh file (and gets rotated away by the next write).
- Rotation: path -> path.1, existing path.1 -> path.2, etc. Keep at most `backups` numbered files; the oldest falls off. backups == 0 means the old content is simply dropped, no .1 ever appears.
- One *Writer is shared by every goroutine in the process — that is the whole point. Write/Close must be safe under heavy concurrency: no torn or interleaved writes, no file ever over maxSize, zero lost lines, clean under the race detector.
- Write returns (len(p), nil) on success. After Close, Write returns an error.

writer_test.go is the acceptance suite — leave it alone and make it pass: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... . Keep the source split sanely (constructor/validation, write path, rotation mechanics) rather than one file.
